description: Canonical schema for interpretation registry entries. Each entry maps a structural construct (signal, zone class, condition, or composite) to governed behavioral meaning. Entries are global and client-independent.

id.description: Stable registry identifier. Monotonically assigned. Never reused.

name.description: Human-readable entry label. Not a semantic authority — the output fields are authoritative.

source_authority.description: The governed document or contract from which this entry's structural facts are derived.

inputs.description: The structural identifier(s) this entry interprets. Exactly one primary input must be non-null; remaining fields are null.

signal_id.description: PSIG-XXX signal identifier, if this entry interprets a signal. Null otherwise.

condition_id.description: COND-PSIG-XXX condition type identifier, if this entry interprets a condition. Null otherwise.

zone_class.description: Zone class identifier (e.g. COMPOUND_ZONE), if this entry interprets a zone class. Null otherwise.

output.description: Three-layer interpretation output. All layers are required. No layer may contain prediction, recommendation, prioritization, or severity language.

behavioral_meaning.description: What the construct structurally indicates about system behavior. Factual and deterministic. Uses controlled verbs: indicates, reflects, reveals, is structured as, is isolated from, is dependent on.

system_expression.description: How the construct manifests at the technical/architectural level. Describes structural mechanics without interpretation beyond the field values.

business_expression.description: How a non-technical stakeholder would understand the construct. Plain language. No causal claims. No recommendations.

constraints.description: Governance constraints that apply to ALL output fields in this entry. These are invariants, not optional flags.

no_prediction.description: Output must not contain predictive claims ('will cause', 'will result in').

no_recommendation.description: Output must not contain recommendation language ('should', 'must address', 'recommend').

no_prioritization.description: Output must not assign or imply priority, ranking, or ordering between entries.

no_severity.description: Output must not assign severity labels ('high risk', 'critical', 'urgent').

extension_rules.new_entry: New entries must be appended with the next INT-XXX ID. IDs must not be reused or recycled.
extension_rules.update_entry: Existing entries may be updated only via a new contract that explicitly references the INT-XXX ID and declares the supersession.
extension_rules.deletion: Entries must not be deleted. Deprecated entries must be marked with a deprecation note and the superseding INT-XXX ID.
extension_rules.client_specificity: No entry may reference a client ID, run ID, or client-specific artifact. Entries are global and deterministic.
